briefs.title: Delta Council Approves Highways Bylaws and Votes on Townhouse Rezoning
briefs.summary: At its April 13, 2026 meeting, Delta Council voted on several items affecting local businesses and residents, including final approval of updated Highways Bylaws — rules governing roads and ticketing — and first readings for a rezoning that would allow a nine-unit townhouse at 4984 45 Avenue. Council also passed motions to advocate for better primary care access in Delta and to support the Royal Canadian Legion's 100th anniversary celebration.
briefs.action_required: If you own property or operate near 4984 45 Avenue, monitor the rezoning process — a final vote is still to come.

briefs.title: Council to consider: Delta approves its April 13, 2026 meeting agenda
briefs.summary: Delta Council is set to vote on endorsing the official agenda for its April 13, 2026 Regular Council meeting. This is a routine procedural step — it simply confirms the list of items Council will discuss that day and does not involve any new rules or requirements for businesses.

briefs.title: Council to Consider: Receiving Closed Meeting Items into the Record
briefs.summary: Delta Council is set to vote on formally receiving items from its March 30, 2026 closed (in-camera) meeting into the official public record. A closed meeting is a private session where councillors discuss confidential matters. This is a routine procedural step and does not signal any specific new policy or bylaw affecting businesses at this time.

briefs.title: Council to Consider: Delta Consent Agenda Items 6.1 to 6.4
briefs.summary: Delta Council is set to vote on adopting Items 6.1 to 6.4 as a group under its consent agenda — a process where multiple routine items are approved together in a single vote rather than debated one by one. The specific content of Items 6.1 to 6.4 was not included in the available agenda text, so the direct impact on local businesses is not yet clear.
briefs.action_required: Check the full Delta Council agenda for Items 6.1–6.4 to see if any affect your business.

key_dates.label: Delta Council consent agenda vote — Items 6.1 to 6.4

briefs.title: Council to vote on approving minutes from Delta's closed March 30, 2026 meeting
briefs.summary: Delta Council is set to vote on formally approving the minutes from a special closed-door meeting held on March 30, 2026. That meeting was held 'in-camera' — meaning it was closed to the public — to discuss private matters such as staff appointments, a potential land deal, legal advice, and confidential talks with other governments.

briefs.title: Council to Approve: Delta March 30, 2026 Meeting Minutes Including BIA and Zoning Bylaws
briefs.summary: Delta Council is set to vote on adopting the minutes from its March 30, 2026 Regular Council meeting. Those minutes record several decisions already made at that meeting, including the final adoption of the Tsawwassen Business Improvement Area Bylaw, zoning changes at two residential sites, and first readings of a new Highways Bylaw — any of which could affect local business owners.
briefs.action_required: Review the Tsawwassen BIA Bylaw and new Highways Bylaw details to see if your business is affected.

briefs.title: Council to Receive Update: Delta's 2026 Transit Infrastructure Improvements Program
briefs.summary: Delta Council is set to receive a report on the City's 2026 transit infrastructure improvements program. The program covers 15 new bus shelters, 20 benches, and accessibility upgrades at 5 bus stops across Delta's industrial areas and residential neighbourhoods, with a total budget of $750,000.
briefs.action_required: If your business is near an affected bus stop, watch for construction activity at your location.

briefs.title: Council to Receive Delta Diversity Committee's 2023–2025 Achievements Report
briefs.summary: Delta Council is set to formally receive a report summarizing what the Diversity, Inclusion and Anti-Racism Committee (DIAC) accomplished between 2023 and 2025. The report highlights initiatives such as the inaugural DIYAFest, free menstrual products in city facilities, newcomer settlement supports, and inclusive housing work — but no new rules or requirements for businesses are being proposed.
briefs.action_required: No action needed — this report is informational only and does not create new rules for businesses.

briefs.title: Delta Council Adopts New Highways Bylaw with Updated Ticketing Rules
briefs.summary: At its April 13, 2026 meeting, Delta Council voted to finally adopt three bylaws updating the rules for highways (public roads) in Delta, including how fines and bylaw notices are issued to those who break the rules. These bylaws are now in effect, meaning businesses that use, access, or operate near Delta's public roads should be aware that new ticketing and enforcement rules apply.
briefs.action_required: Review the new Delta Highways Bylaw to check if any rules affect your business's road access or deliveries.

briefs.title: Council to vote on: Delta Highways Bylaw update affecting roads and enforcement
briefs.summary: Delta Council is set to vote on final adoption of three updated bylaws covering highways, ticketing, and bylaw enforcement. If approved, the changes would update the rules for how Delta's roads and public rights-of-way are managed, align enforcement language across related bylaws, and strengthen tools for issuing fines (tickets) to those who break the rules.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the April 13 council decision — new road-use rules and fines could affect your business operations.

key_dates.label: Delta Highways Bylaw No. 8452 — final adoption vote at council meeting

briefs.title: Council to Consider Rezoning for Nine-Unit Townhouse Development in Delta
briefs.summary: At its April 13, 2026 meeting, Delta Council gave first, second, and third readings — early approval steps before a bylaw becomes law — to a zoning change that would allow a nine-unit townhouse development at 4984 45 Avenue. The proposal still requires the property owner to meet several conditions, including signing legal agreements, posting financial securities, and paying a $9,000 community amenity contribution, before final approval is granted.
briefs.action_required: If this development affects your business or property, monitor Delta Council's agenda for the final adoption vote.

briefs.title: Proposed: Delta Council to Vote on Nine-Unit Townhouse Rezoning at 4984 45 Avenue
briefs.summary: Delta Council is set to vote on a rezoning application that would allow a nine-unit townhouse development at 4984 45 Avenue in Ladner. The proposal would change the property's zoning from single detached/duplex residential to townhouse residential, and includes several variance requests — meaning the developer is asking for exceptions to standard rules around parking, building spacing, and landscaping buffers.
briefs.action_required: Attend the April 13, 2026 Delta Council meeting to share your views before the vote.

key_dates.label: Delta Council meeting — rezoning vote for 4984 45 Avenue townhouse development
Provincial Housing Target Order — Delta occupancy deadline for units to count

briefs.title: Council to Consider: Rezoning and Variance Application for 4984 45 Avenue, Ladner
briefs.summary: Delta Council will consider a rezoning and development variance permit application (File LU009863) submitted by Hardeep Bassi of Bassi Properties for a property at 4984 45 Avenue in Ladner. Residents have written to Council opposing the proposal, raising concerns about the large number of bylaw variances (exceptions to existing rules) requested, high occupancy density, reduced spacing between buildings, and insufficient parking for future residents and visitors.
briefs.action_required: Attend the Delta Council meeting on April 13, 2026 to voice your support or concerns about this application.

key_dates.label: Delta Council meeting — LU009863 rezoning and variance application considered

briefs.title: Council to Consider Cancelling and Rewriting Houseplex Zoning Bylaw in Delta
briefs.summary: Delta Council is set to vote on cancelling (rescinding) Bylaw No. 8499, a zoning rule that would have allowed four-unit houseplexes on four new lots at 809 51 Street and 5140 8A Avenue. The original bylaw contained errors in how building size and height were calculated, so staff want to scrap it and bring back a corrected version for Council to consider at a future meeting.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the revised Bylaw No. 8499 public notice — you can comment before Council votes on it.

briefs.title: Delta Council Approves Legion Centennial Support and Pushes for More Primary Care
briefs.summary: At its April 13, 2026 meeting, Delta Council voted to support the Royal Canadian Legion Branch 61's 100th anniversary celebration and to install two commemorative crosswalks, pending outside funding. Council also approved a plan to advocate for better primary care access in Delta — including same-day urgent care and more family doctors — and to share a needs assessment report with health authorities, local MLAs, and the Minister of Health.
briefs.action_required: If you employ staff or serve customers in Delta, watch for updates on local healthcare capacity improvements.

briefs.title: Council to Consider Supporting Royal Canadian Legion Branch 61's 100th Anniversary and Commemorative Crosswalks
briefs.summary: Delta Council is set to vote on providing up to $10,000 in financial and in-kind support for the Royal Canadian Legion Branch 61's 100th Anniversary Celebration on July 25, 2026, and on installing two commemorative crosswalks in Ladner and North Delta to honour local veterans. The crosswalks — estimated at $40,000 total — would only proceed if the Legion secures roughly $24,000 in federal grant funding, with Delta covering the remaining costs.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the April 13, 2026 council decision if your business operates near the Ladner or North Delta event areas.

key_dates.label: Delta Council meeting — vote on Legion support and commemorative crosswalks
Royal Canadian Legion Branch 61 100th Anniversary Celebration event date

briefs.title: Council to Consider: Delta Advocates for More Family Doctors and a Teaching Clinic
briefs.summary: Delta Council is set to vote on two proposals to tackle the city's primary care shortage. The first would have the City formally push the provincial Ministry of Health to expand same-day urgent care access and recruit more family doctors. The second would direct staff to explore a 'teaching clinic' model — a clinic where medical students train while treating patients — with potential university, Fraser Health, and provincial partners.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — this could affect healthcare access and staffing for health-sector businesses in Delta.

briefs.title: Delta Council April 13 Meeting: No New Motions Proposed
briefs.summary: At the April 13, 2026 Delta Council meeting, no new motions were put forward under the Notice of Motions agenda item. The meeting did cover other items, including final adoption of updated highways bylaws, a rezoning application for a nine-unit townhouse, and a primary care advocacy resolution — but no new proposals were tabled for future consideration.

briefs.title: Delta Council April 13 Meeting: Highways Bylaws Adopted, Healthcare Advocacy Approved
briefs.summary: At its April 13, 2026 meeting, Delta Council voted on several items — including final adoption of updated highways bylaws that set new rules for roads and ticketing, and a motion to push the provincial Ministry of Health to expand urgent and primary care services in Delta. These decisions are now on the record as approved by Council.
briefs.action_required: Check the new Delta Highways Bylaw rules if your business involves road use, deliveries, or signage on public roads.
